blocks.value: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the total market value of all final goods and services produced within a country in a given period. The expenditure approach adds four components: Consumption (C), Investment (I), Government spending (G), and Net exports — exports minus imports (X - M).

blocks.caption: GDP by expenditure: C + I + G + (X - M).

items.q: Which is NOT a component of GDP by expenditure?

items.back: Market value of all final goods and services produced in a country in a period.
Goods sold to the end user, not resold as inputs.

blocks.value: GDP measures final output produced inside a country. By expenditure, GDP = C + I + G + (X - M).
